behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is between manifest validation and plugin execution path: the loader must validate manifest metadata and short-circuit before import/onLoad. A pure unit test of only `validateManifest` would miss regressions where loader still imports module despite validation failure. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is about local loader flow (`manifest.json`, reject, no execution), with no real network/subprocess/OS-dependent artifact and no AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with an invalid `manifest.json` and an entry module that writes a side-effect marker if executed. Run loader initialization over this directory; assert loader reports validation rejection and side-effect marker is absent (module never executed).

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when dependency resolver output is consumed by lifecycle invoker; this is a module handoff issue, not just single-function logic. Unit tests of topo-sort alone miss hook-call ordering bugs in orchestration. Higher levels rejected: behavior only requires local module wiring and call-order observation; no subprocess/network/OS-specific behavior and no AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Set up two plugins where A depends on B, each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared in-memory log. Run loader and assert call log order is `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Need combined behavior of cycle detection + load suppression. A unit test of cycle detection algorithm alone would not catch a loader bug that still loads one plugin after detecting a cycle. Higher levels rejected: expected artifact is local contract/state mismatch (error report and zero loads), not environment-dependent behavior; no AI/model output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and `onLoad` hooks that log execution. Initialize loader; assert a cycle error is reported and neither hook log entry exists.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across module import/execution and lifecycle invocation. Unit-testing hook invoker alone cannot catch regressions where `onLoad` is called before entry-module side effects run. Higher levels rejected: behavior references local Node import semantics within same process; no real external infra requirement (no HTTP, subprocess, OS event dependence) and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin entry module that sets `globalThis.pluginInitialized = true` on import and exports `onLoad` that asserts this flag is already true (or records observed state). Run loader and assert `onLoad` sees post-import state.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Requires interaction of error handling, dependency graph filtering, and continued lifecycle execution across multiple plugins. Unit test of try/catch around one hook would miss dependency-aware continuation logic. Higher levels rejected: failure is orchestration logic among local modules; no requirement for subprocess/network/OS variability; no AI/ML output or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Set up plugins: F (throws in `onLoad`), D (depends on F), and I (independent). Run loader; assert F failure is reported, D is skipped/not loaded due to dependency, and I `onLoad` still runs successfully.
